data.title: Cadeler signs deal to install foundations at large offshore wind farm in Europe in 2028.
data.description: In Denmark, Cadeler has secured a contract to transport and install monopiles and transition pieces for a major offshore wind project in Europe. The agreement involves the deployment of key components at the wind farm site, indicating significant progress in the renewable energy sector.

data.title: Stolt-Nielsen Limited discloses 2025 Q4 & full year unaudited results.
data.description: In a recent report, it was stated that the unemployment rate in the United States had decreased to 4.8% in the month of October. This marks a significant improvement from the previous month, where the rate stood at 5.2%. Additionally, the economy added 531,000 jobs in October, with notable gains in the sectors of hospitality, retail, and professional services. The strong job growth and decline in unemployment have been attributed to various factors, including the easing of COVID-19 restrictions and increased consumer confidence. Despite these positive indicators, concerns remain about ongoing inflation and supply chain issues impacting the overall economic recovery. Experts suggest that continued monitoring and targeted interventions will be necessary to sustain and further improve the current economic conditions in the country.

data.title: ZetaDisplay partners with LG to preload Engage Suite on professional displays for faster deployment and reduced complexity.
data.description: ZetaDisplay and LG have teamed up to introduce a new line of professional displays that come preloaded with the Engage Suite. This collaboration aims to streamline the process of deploying digital signage solutions, making it faster and less complex for businesses. The factory preloading of the Engage Suite on these displays will enable faster setup and reduce the overall complexity of implementing digital signage. This move is expected to make digital signage more accessible to businesses of all sizes, offering a scalable solution that can cater to varying needs.

data.title: Torben Bender nominated to MT Højgaard's board of directors, replacing Pernille Fabricius after 12 years.Meeting on 20 March.
data.description: During the upcoming general meeting on March 20, 2026, the board of directors of MT Højgaard Holding A/S plans to suggest the appointment of Torben Bender, current CEO of Copenhagen Infrastructure Capital (CI Capital), to join the board. If approved, Bender will take over from Pernille Fabricius, who has been on the board for 12 years and will be resigning during this meeting.

This update is expected to significantly benefit the customer base, which currently stands at [insert number] users. data.title: WinDC partners with Megaport, connecting renewable AI Factories to global NaaS platform, introducing new AI infrastructure in Australia.
data.description: WinDC has recently entered into a significant partnership with Megaport (ASX:MP1) for connecting their renewable-powered AI Factories to Megaport's global Network as a Service platform. This collaboration intends to link high-density AI compute resources stationed at renewable generation sites with organizations across Australia and on Megaport's worldwide network. Through this partnership, WinDC aims to introduce a novel category of AI infrastructure in the Australian market, facilitating access to innovative AI capabilities on a large scale.

data.title: Volvo Group's 2025 net sales reached SEK 479.2 billion, adjusted operating income SEK 51.2 billion. Operating margin 10.7%.
data.description: In Gothenburg, Sweden, the Volvo Group reported net sales of SEK 479.2 billion during 2025. The adjusted operating income for the same period was SEK 51.2 billion, with an adjusted operating margin of 10.7%. This marked a decrease compared to the previous year, where the operating income was SEK 65.7 billion, and the operating margin stood at 12.5%. The return on capital employed for the year...

data.title: Finland real estate market in 2026 split into growth and challenged sectors, driven by digital infrastructure and Lapland tourism.
data.description: In Helsinki on January 28, 2026, it was reported that Finland's real estate market is projected to experience a divergent recovery in the upcoming year. The market is expected to divide into sectors exhibiting structural growth and those encountering long-term obstacles. Trevian Asset Management highlighted this trend, indicating that the market's trajectory will no longer be driven by a singular direction. This shift suggests that certain segments of the real estate market will thrive while others will continue to face challenges. The forecast underscores the complex landscape of Finland's real estate sector in 2026.
